dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4908 IS: Gas Money Saved Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Markey introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To require the Administrator of the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration to initiate a process to reevaluate corporate average fuel economy standards, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Gas Money Saved Act.

paragraph.text: If the Administrator of the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, using data published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, determines that national average prices for gasoline have risen at least 5 times faster than overall inflation over the applicable period described in paragraph (2), the Administrator shall initiate a process to reevaluate whether the current corporate average fuel economy standards are achieving maximum feasible average fuel economy.
The applicable period referred to in paragraph (1) is—

subparagraph.text: if corporate average fuel economy standards have not previously been adjusted under this subsection, any period of 180 days or longer beginning after the date of enactment of this Act; or
if corporate average fuel economy standards have previously been adjusted under this subsection, any period of 180 days or longer beginning after the date of the most recent adjustment.

subsection.text: Section 32912 of title 49, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (b), in the matter preceding paragraph (1), by striking $0.00 and inserting $25; and
in subsection (c)(1)(B), by striking $0.00 and inserting $50.
